item.title: Neural Correlates of Conﬂicting Social Inﬂuence on Adolescent Risk Taking
item.description: Citation: Kwon, S-J., Do, K. T., McCormick, E. M., & Telzer, E. H. (2020). Neural correlates of conflicting social influence on adolescent risk-taking. Journal of Research on Adolescence, 1-14.

item.title: Neural sensitivity to conflicting attitudes supports greater conformity toward positive over negative influence in early adolescence
item.description: Citation: Do, K. T., McCormick, E. M., & Telzer, E. H. (2020). Neural sensitivity to conflicting attitudes supports greater conformity toward positive over negative influence in early adolescence. Developmental Cognitive Neuroscience, 45, 100837.

item.title: Differential behavioral and neural profiles in youth with conduct problems during risky decision‐making
item.description: Citation: van Hoorn, J., McCormick, E. M., Perino, M. T., Rogers, C. R., & Telzer, E. H. (2020). Differential behavioral and neural profiles in youth with conduct problems during risky decisionmaking. Journal of Research on Adolescence, 1-15.

item.title: Maternal emotion socialization in early childhood predicts adolescents’ amygdala-vmPFC functional connectivity to emotion faces
item.description: Citation: Chen, X., McCormick, E. M., Ravindran, N., McElwain, N. L., & Telzer, E. H. (2020). Maternal emotion socialization in early childhood predicts adolescents’ amygdala-vmPFC functional connectivity to emotion faces. Developmental Psychology, 56(3), 503-515.

item.title: Apples to apples? Neural correlates of emotion regulation differences between high and low risk adolescents
item.description: Citation: Perino, M. T., Guassi Moreira, J. F., McCormick, E. M., & Telzer, E. H. (2019). The neural development of prosocial behavior from childhood to adolescence. Social Cognitive and Affective Neuroscience, 14(2), 129-139.

item.title: Behavioral and neural dysregulation to social rewards and links to internalizing symptoms in adolescents
item.description: Citation: Kwon, S-J., Ivory, S. L., McCormick, E. M., & Telzer, E. H. (2019). Behavioral and neural dysregulation to social rewards and links to internalizing symptoms in adolescents. Frontiers in Behavioral Neuroscience, 13, 158.

item.title: Alterations in adolescent dopaminergic systems as a function of early mother-toddler attachment: a prospective longitudinal examination
item.description: Citation: McCormick, E. M., McElwain, N. A., & Telzer, E. H. (2019). Alterations in adolescent dopaminergic systems as a function of early mother-toddler attachment: a prospective longitudinal examination. International Journal of Developmental Neuroscience, 78, 122-129 .

item.title: The neural development of prosocial behavior from childhood to adolescence
item.description: Citation: Do, K. T., McCormick, E. M., & Telzer, E. H. (2019). The neural development of prosocial behavior from childhood to adolescence. Social Cognitive and Affective Neuroscience, 14(2), 129-139.

item.title: Model-based network discovery of developmental and performance-related differences during risky decision-making
item.description: Citation: McCormick, E. M., Gates, K. M., & Telzer, E. H. (2018). Model-based network discovery of developmental and performance-related differences during risky decision-making. NeuroImage, 188, 456-464 .

item.title: Contributions of default mode network stability and deactivation to adolescent task engagement
item.description: Citation: McCormick, E. M., & Telzer, E. H. (2018). Contributions of default mode network stability and deactivation to adolescent task engagement. Scientific Reports, 8, 18049.

item.title: Differential effects of parent and peer presence on neural correlates of risk taking in adolescence
item.description: Citation: van Hoorn, J., McCormick, E. M., Rogers, C. R., Ivory, S., & Telzer, E. H. (2018). Differential effects of parent and peer presence on neural correlates of risk taking in adolescence. Social Cognitive and Affective Neuroscience, 13(9), 945–955.

item.title: Neural correlates of sibling closeness and association with externalizing behavior in adolescence
item.description: Citation: Rogers, C. R., McCormick, E. M., van Hoorn, J., Ivory, S., & Telzer, E. H. (2018). Neural correlates of sibling closeness and association with externalizing behavior in adolescence. Social Cognitive and Affective Neuroscience, 13, 977–988.

item.title: Subjective social status and neural processing of race in Mexican-American adolescents
item.description: Citation: Muscatell, K. A., McCormick, E. M., & Telzer, E. H. (2018). Subjective social status and neural processing of race in Mexican-American adolescents. Development and Psychopathology, 5, 1–12.

item.title: Moderate social sensitivity in a risky context supports adaptive decision-making in adolescence: Evidence from brain and behavior
item.description: Citation: van Hoorn, J., McCormick, E. M., & Telzer, E. H. (2018). Moderate social sensitivity in a risky context supports adaptive decision-making in adolescence: Evidence from brain and behavior. Social Cognitive and Affective Neuroscience, 13(5), 546–556.

item.title: Methodological considerations for longitudinal fMRI research
item.description: Citation: Telzer, E. H., McCormick, E. M., Peters, S., Cosme, D., Pfeifer, P. H., & van Duijvenvoorde, A. C. K. (2018). Methodological considerations for longitudinal fMRI research. Developmental Cognitive Neuroscience, 33, 149–160.

item.title: Not just social sensitivity: Adolescent neural suppression of social feedback during risk taking
item.description: Citation: McCormick, E. M., Perino, M. T., & Telzer, E. H. (2018). Not just social sensitivity: Adolescent neural suppression of social feedback during risk taking. Developmental Cognitive Neuroscience, 30, 134–141.

item.title: Not doomed to repeat: Enhanced mPFC tracking of errors promotes adaptive behavior during adolescence
item.description: Citation: McCormick, E. M., & Telzer, E. H. (2018). Not doomed to repeat: Enhanced mPFC tracking of errors promotes adaptive behavior during adolescence. Journal of Cognitive Neuroscience, 30(3), 281–289.

item.title: Youth's conceptions of adolescence predict longitudinal changes in prefrontal cortex activation and risk taking
item.description: Citation: Qu, Y., Pomerantz, E. M., McCormick, E. M., & Telzer, E. H. (2018). Youth’s conceptions of adolescence predict longitudinal changes in prefrontal cortex activation and risk taking. Child Development, 89, 773-783.

item.title: Failure to retreat: Blunted sensitivity to negative feedback supports risky behavior in adolescents
item.description: Citation: McCormick, E. M., & Telzer, E. H. (2017). Failure to retreat: Blunted sensitivity to negative feedback supports risky behavior in adolescents. NeuroImage, 147, 381-389.

item.title: Adaptive adolescent flexibility: Neurodevelopment of decision-making and learning in a risky context
item.description: Citation: McCormick, E. M., & Telzer, E. H. (2017). Adaptive adolescent flexibility: Neurodevelopment of decision-making and learning in a risky context. Journal of Cognitive Neuroscience, 29(3), 413-423.

item.title: Activation in context: Differential conclusions drawn from cross-sectional and longitudinal analyses of adolescents’ cognitive control-related neural activity
item.description: Citation: McCormick, E. M., Qu, Y., & Telzer, E. H. (2017). Activation in context: Differential conclusions drawn from cross-sectional and longitudinal analyses of adolescents’ cognitive control-related neural activity. Frontiers in Human Neuroscience, 11, 141.
